responsibilities: Give instructions through Telegram or dashboard.
Approve client-facing delivery, filing, signature and Workspace writeback gates.
Receive concise partner briefings from Hermes.
Receive the lawyer's instruction and resolve client, matter, process and urgency.
Create command records, matter briefs and delegation plans.
Control Paperclip staff, Workspace artifacts, approvals and final reporting.
Execute assigned legal work through the 14 firm roles.
Return source-backed artifacts, blockers, missing data and review decisions.
Never bypass Hermes or mark client delivery complete without required gates.
Store sources, transcripts, live files, control master rows, drafts, review packages and deliverables.
Expose Drive, Docs, Sheets, Calendar and Tasks state through approved adapters.
Keep local fallback shapes compatible when live credentials are unavailable.
Show the lawyer what Hermes is directing, who is working, where the work lives and what needs approval.
Represent actions as instructions to Hermes, not direct raw system writes.
Make unauthorized, blocked and pending states obvious.

lifecycleStages.description: Hermes receives the lawyer's instruction from Telegram or dashboard.
Hermes resolves client, matter, live memory, legal process and urgency.
Hermes writes an auditable command record and matter brief.
Hermes creates Paperclip staff assignments with role-specific context packages.
Paperclip workers produce required legal artifacts and blockers.
Workspace stores sources, drafts, control rows, reviews and deliverables.
Hermes enforces senior review, delivery, filing, signature and writeback gates.
Hermes reports status, links, blockers and next asks to the lawyer.

dashboardPrinciples: Hermes is shown as the managing partner directing the office.
Paperclip workers are shown as legal staff, not abstract agents.
Workspace is shown as the office where the work lives.
Every action is an instruction to Hermes unless explicitly marked read-only.
